ops.title: Workflow scripts interpolate `${...}` normally at script runtime — never write `${'${'}...}` escapes in agent() prompts; subagents receive literal unfilled placeholders
ops.body:
In `Workflow` tool scripts, agent() prompts are plain JS template literals evaluated at script runtime: `${SCOPE}` and `${f.claim}` interpolate correctly. Writing `${'${'}SCOPE}` (over-escaping, as if the string needed to survive another evaluation layer) sends the literal text `${SCOPE}` to the subagent.

**Why:** In the 2026-07-16 hunk-ship review run, every refuter received `CLAIM [${f.severity}] ${f.title}` with no actual claim — five subagents messaged back asking for their missing input, and the whole Find/Refute phase had to be stopped, fixed (one `replace_all` of `${'${'}` → `${`), and re-run. Earlier phases' gate stages silently lost their advisory context the same way (survivable only because they were told to read the tree directly).

**How to apply:** Write normal `${expr}` inside Workflow script prompts. If a literal `${` must appear in a prompt, escape it as `\${`. Symptom to watch for: a subagent replying that its prompt contains unrendered `${...}` placeholders — stop the run, fix the script file, resume from the run ID. Related: [[per-occurrence-rollout-traps]].
